text:
Both spec files and data now read. Here's exactly what I'll build for `system/specs/demo-notice.md`: **Head (JSON fence):** - `component`: `"demo-notice"` — matches filename stem. - `status`: `"spec"` — no CSS ships yet; ticket #8 is the one that lands the CSS and flips this to `"shipped"` (mirrors primary-button's honesty-surface convention, just the other side of it). - `class`: `"vd-demo-notice"` — Verdant prefix, joins the pack `gen-handoff.mjs` emits; Fieldwork gets its own class when its ticket lands. - `contract`: `null` — presentational, same as primary-button; the composing page owns the scenario and passes the notice string down as a prop, no API record binds here. - `props`: one field, `text: { type: "string", required: true, description: "the scenario's fictionalNotice string, rendered verbatim — never paraphrased or summarized" }`. Kept to exactly one prop — this component has one job. - `tokens`: a small, honest set for a quiet caption-style notice — `--color-fg-muted` (the notice text itself must read as secondary, not alarming), `--color-bg-surface`, `--color-border`, `--type-caption`, `--spacing-sm`. All five exist verbatim in the `contract` group of tokens.source.json. - `states`: `["default"]` — it's a static disclosure, not interactive; one state is honest given nothing changes. - `children`: `[]`. **Prose (four required sections, in order):** - `## Usage` — states this is honesty surface #1: the visible label telling a viewer the scenario (Verdant, Fieldwork, …) is fictional. Renders whatever `fictionalNotice` string the scenario's copy.json carries; composing page is responsible for scenario selection, this component only displays. - `## States` — only `default`; notes plainly that no CSS exists yet (status: spec) so today it's unstyled semantic markup, and that ticket #8 is what gives it visual treatment. - `## Data binding` — presentational like primary-button: a one-row prop mapping table, `text` → the rendered notice content, sourced from `scenario.copy.fictionalNotice`, required (never legitimately absent — a demo without this notice would be a real honesty problem). - `## Accessibility` — plain-text semantic element (e.g. a `

` or `role="note"`), always in the accessibility tree and never visually hidden or truncated — the whole point is that assistive tech users get the same disclosure as sighted ones; `--color-fg-muted` on `--color-bg-surface` still needs to clear contrast even though it reads as "secondary." That's the full spec: minimal props, minimal tokens, minimal states — as small as an honest disclosure component can be.

input.description: noop placeholder, no exploration in plan phase

text:
Checking the plan against kb-format.md's head schema line by line: `component` must equal filename stem — `demo-notice` matches `demo-notice.md`, correct. `status` must be `spec | shipped` — I used `spec`, valid enum value, and it's honest: ticket #8 is stated by the task as the one shipping CSS, so `spec` is right, not `shipped`. `class` must carry the `vd-` prefix for Verdant — `vd-demo-notice` is correct and matches the naming pattern in primary-button (`vd-primary-button`). `contract` is `null` for presentational components — the task explicitly says contract: null, and my reasoning (composing page passes the string) matches primary-button's identical pattern, so this is consistent, not just asserted.

`props` — kb-format requires `{ type, required, enum?, description? }` per prop. My single `text` prop has `type`, `required`, `description` — no `enum` needed since it's freeform scenario copy, so omitting it is fine (it's marked optional with `?`). One catch: I should double check the exact shape used in primary-button — it used `type`, `required`, `description`, no `enum`, matching mine. Good, format aligns exactly.

`tokens` — must each be present in the `contract` group of tokens.source.json, `--`-prefixed. Let me verify each of my five choices against what I actually read: `color-fg-muted` ✓ (fg-surface group), `color-bg-surface` ✓ (fg-surface group), `color-border` ✓ (fg-surface group), `type-caption` ✓ (type-ramp group), `spacing-sm` ✓ (spacing group). All five exist verbatim in the contract group as read. I need to prefix them with `--` in the actual JSON array, matching primary-button's `"--color-accent"` style — I'll make sure to write them as `--color-fg-muted` etc., not bare names.

`states` — non-empty; `["default"]` satisfies that. One legitimate concern: is a single "default" state too thin compared to primary-button's four states? But primary-button is interactive (hover/pressed/disabled apply); demo-notice is a static text disclosure with no interaction and no CSS yet — a single state is the honest reflection of reality, not corner-cutting. I'm keeping it, but flagging it here as the correct call rather than an oversight.

`children` — `[]`, correct, no composed components.

Prose sections — kb-format requires exactly `## Usage`, `## States`, `## Data binding`, `## Accessibility`, in that order, all four required. My plan has all four in that order. Good.

Honesty contract check: this component's entire purpose IS an honesty surface (the fictional-scenario disclosure), so I need to be extra careful the spec doesn't overstate anything. Re-reading my planned Usage section — it correctly says "composing page is responsible for scenario selection, this component only displays," which keeps the honesty contract clean (no unearned claims about what the component decides). Re-reading Data binding — I said the field is `scenario.copy.fictionalNotice`; verified against copy.json which does have exactly that key at the top level (`"fictionalNotice": "Verdant is a fictional product…"`). Correct, not fabricated.

One thing to correct: I need to double check whether the `props` field name should be `text` or should mirror the source key `notice`/`fictionalNotice` more closely for clarity to whoever reads the handoff pack. Looking again at primary-button, its prop names are short and generic (`label`, `disabled`) rather than mirroring internal data field names — so `text` is consistent with the sibling's naming convention. I'll keep `text`.

Also double-check: gen-handoff.mjs "throws on any head violation" — meaning parseComponentSpec in agent-layer/lib.mjs is strict. I haven't read agent-layer/lib.mjs itself, only kb-format.md's description of it. There's a risk of an undocumented strictness (e.g. required top-level key ordering, or additional keys not allowed). Since the task's explicit read-list didn't include lib.mjs or gen-handoff.mjs, I did not read them in the plan phase per protocol — but nothing stops me from letting the validate-phase command be the actual arbiter: if it throws, I fix. That's exactly what step 4 tells me to do, so this is not a gate failure, just a known unknown to watch for at validate.

No corrections needed to the field values themselves; the one methodological adjustment: I'll write the tokens array with proper `--` prefixes exactly as primary-button does, since my prose above described them without the prefix for readability — that's a phrasing-only distinction, not a plan change.

Nothing in this plan writes CSS or claims capabilities the component doesn't have. Ready to implement.
